title: Polenta Fingers
excerpt: Ingredients 500 ml Polenta 65 Butter 90 ml Chopped mixed herbs such as parsley, rosemary, thyme etc. Flavoured olive oil (plain olive oil is also fine) Freshly ground black pepper Method Cook and season the polenta as indicated on the packet and make sure it's creamy, almost like thick custard. html:
Ingredients

  • 500 ml Polenta
  • 65 Butter
  • 90 ml Chopped mixed herbs such as parsley, rosemary, thyme etc.
  • Flavoured olive oil (plain olive oil is also fine)
  • Freshly ground black pepper

Method

Cook and season the polenta as indicated on the packet and make sure it's creamy, almost like thick custard. Stir in the butter and mixed herbs.

Line a small loaf pan with cling wrap or foil and spray with non-stick spray.

Pour the hot polenta into the prepared pan, smooth the top and cover it too with cling wrap or foil. Put the pan in the fridge so the polenta can set, preferably overnight.

To serve, take the polenta “loaf” out of the pan and remove the wrapping. Cut the polenta into 1 cm slices. Brush each slice on both sides with oil.

Heat a pan until hot and fry the slices until golden brown. Don't fry too many slices at once and don't turn the slices more than once.

Keep the fried slices warm until everything is fried and serve.

Makes 6 servings with any meat or vegetarian stew.

Easy!
